# pycubrid

> Pure Python DB-API 2.0 driver for the CUBRID database. Provides connection management, cursor operations, LOB handling, and type mapping with full PEP 249 compliance.

pycubrid is a pure Python driver that communicates with CUBRID database servers using the CAS (CUBRID Application Server) protocol. It supports Python 3.10+ and provides complete DB-API 2.0 compliance including parameterized queries, transactions, LOB operations, and batch inserts.

## Docs

- [Quick Start](https://cubrid-lab.github.io/pycubrid/quickstart/): Connect to CUBRID in 5 minutes with installation, basic connection, and CRUD examples
- [Connection Guide](https://cubrid-lab.github.io/pycubrid/CONNECTION/): Connection parameters, authentication, connection string format, and connection management
- [API Reference](https://cubrid-lab.github.io/pycubrid/API_REFERENCE/): Complete API documentation for connect(), Connection, Cursor, LOB, types, and exceptions
- [Type Mapping](https://cubrid-lab.github.io/pycubrid/TYPES/): CUBRID SQL types to Python type mapping, type constructors, and DBAPIType
- [Examples](https://cubrid-lab.github.io/pycubrid/EXAMPLES/): Practical code examples for common database operations

## Optional

- [Protocol](https://cubrid-lab.github.io/pycubrid/PROTOCOL/): CAS protocol implementation details
- [Architecture](https://cubrid-lab.github.io/pycubrid/ARCHITECTURE/): Internal architecture and module structure
- [Performance](https://cubrid-lab.github.io/pycubrid/PERFORMANCE/): Performance optimization tips
- [Troubleshooting](https://cubrid-lab.github.io/pycubrid/TROUBLESHOOTING/): Common issues and solutions
- [FAQ](https://cubrid-lab.github.io/pycubrid/faq/): Frequently asked questions
- [Support Matrix](https://cubrid-lab.github.io/pycubrid/SUPPORT_MATRIX/): Python and CUBRID version compatibility
- [Development](https://cubrid-lab.github.io/pycubrid/DEVELOPMENT/): Development setup and contributing
